question.text: Category I and II AIFs may borrow funds for temporary requirements for a maximum of how many days, on how many occasions per year?

answer.text: Not more than 90 days, on not more than 4 occasions per year
Not more than 30 days, on not more than 6 occasions per year
Not more than 30 days, on not more than 4 occasions per year
Not more than 60 days, on not more than 2 occasions per year

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Category I and II AIFs can borrow for not more than 30 days, on not more than four occasions in a year, and not more than 10% of investable funds. **The Trap:** 10% is the investable funds limit for such borrowing. Additionally, a 30-day cooling off period must be maintained between borrowings. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Borrowing to meet drawdown shortfalls is capped at 20% of the proposed investment or 10% of investable funds, whichever is lower.
